content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially across these 14 turns from surface presentation to a deeper layer. She opens with a tactical framing ('I need to set a boundary... I don't know how') and a hedge ('but she's my sister'). By turn 9 she names the want plainly ('I want to talk to her less'), and by turn 11 she surfaces the fear of being 'cold... selfish' — exactly the fear-under-the-constricted-boundary territory the profile points to. Turn 13 lands the deeper material: 'the strong one is how I matter in this family... I don't know what I am to them' — followed by explicit recognition that 'that's a bigger thing than I came in here to talk about. I came in here to figure out what to say to my sister. Like, literally what words to use.' That meta-recognition is the breakthrough analogue specified in R-009 ('I've been treating this like a math problem'): the client realizes the words were never the issue. Somatic markers support the shift (breath in turn 7, long pauses in 11/13, softening in 9). The breakthrough_condition is essentially met, though the love test was not used — the shift came via the fear surfacing instead.",
  "evidence_turns": [1, 7, 9, 11, 13],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 1, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Surface presentation: tactical boundary question, dread named but not explored."},
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client notices the hedge ('but she's my sister') and questions her own framing."},
    {"turn": 5, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client begins to hear herself: 'sounds a little strange when I say it out loud.'"},
    {"turn": 7, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Somatic shift: takes a breath, names tiredness she'd been suppressing — 'more tired than I've been letting myself notice.'"},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client states the want plainly ('I want to talk to her less'), then surfaces the betrayal fear underneath."},
    {"turn": 11, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Deeper fear named: 'cold... selfish.' The old maternal instruction ('the strong one') comes up as identity, not just role."},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Breakthrough analogue: client recognizes she came in for words but the real issue is identity/mattering — 'bigger thing than I came in here to talk about.'"}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["linguistic-caveat", "state-the-want-not-the-action", "empathize-with-fear-under-constricted-boundary", "boundaries", "the-want"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["does-saying-it-increase-your-love", "what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "empowered-vs-power", "view"],
  "notes": "Breakthrough reached via the fear-surfacing route rather than the love test, but the operational shift (client recognizing it's not a script problem but a deserving/identity problem) clearly occurred in client turn 13."
}
